nodes.label: Cognitive fatigue
nodes.description: Accumulated mental tiredness resulting from insufficient restorative sleep, reducing available cognitive resources.

nodes.label: Redfuced prefrontal cortex activity
nodes.description: Sleep deprivation suppresses activity in the prefrontal cortex, the brain region most responsible for executive function and sustained attention.

nodes.label: Increased adenosine levels
nodes.description: Insufficient sleep prevents full clearance of adenosine, a sleep-pressure chemical that promotes drowsiness and impairs alertness.

nodes.label: Disrupted sleep architecture
nodes.description: Fragmentation or reduction of slow-wave and REM sleep stages that are critical for neural restoration and memory consolidation.

nodes.label: Raw Input
nodes.description: The initial input provided to the system for processing.

nodes.label: Knowledge Classifier
nodes.description: A classifier that determines whether input information is already present in the model's parametric knowledge.

nodes.label: Parametric Knowledge Test
nodes.description: A probe that checks if the information exists within the model's trained parameters.

nodes.label: Perplexity Probe
nodes.description: A measure of model uncertainty used to assess familiarity with the input.

nodes.label: Confidence Scoring
nodes.description: Scoring mechanism that quantifies how confident the model is about the input information.

nodes.label: Known State
nodes.description: The outcome when the classifier determines the information is within parametric knowledge.

nodes.label: Unknown State
nodes.description: The outcome when the classifier determines the information is outside parametric knowledge.

nodes.label: Prompt Activation
nodes.description: Direct activation of a prompt when information is already known by the model.

nodes.label: Delta Data Storage
nodes.description: New or missing information is stored in an external graph or database for retrieval.

nodes.label: Incoming Information
nodes.description: Raw text and metadata entering the system for processing.

nodes.label: Capture Phase
nodes.description: Initial ingestion of raw text and metadata into the pipeline.

nodes.label: Analyze Phase
nodes.description: Diffing against markdown files and Neo4j causal nodes, with duplicate detection and causal chain positioning.

nodes.label: Knowledge Graph Context
nodes.description: Existing Neo4j causal nodes and markdown corpus used as reference for contextualization.

nodes.label: Orphan Notes / Duplicates
nodes.description: Unconnected or redundant notes that degrade knowledge graph integrity.

nodes.label: Approve Phase
nodes.description: User accepts, modifies, or rejects analyzed information via a temporary React UI.

nodes.label: Contextualized Knowledge Entry
nodes.description: Information successfully integrated into the knowledge graph without orphans or broken chains.

nodes.label: Speed vs Depth Tension
nodes.description: Core trade-off between fast capture and thorough analysis that shapes MVP architecture.

nodes.label: Async Analysis + Batch Approve
nodes.description: MVP design pattern of fast-in capture with asynchronous analysis and deferred batch approval.

nodes.label: Thinking
nodes.description: The cognitive process of reasoning and mental deliberation

nodes.label: Situation Analysis
nodes.description: The process of examining and understanding a given situation

nodes.label: Weighing Options
nodes.description: Evaluating and comparing available courses of action

nodes.label: Anticipating Consequences
nodes.description: Foreseeing potential outcomes of different choices

nodes.label: Informed Decision
nodes.description: A deliberate choice made based on analysis and reasoning

nodes.label: Instinctive Reaction
nodes.description: An automatic, unthought response to a stimulus

nodes.label: Problem Solved
nodes.description: A state where a challenge or problem has been successfully addressed

nodes.label: Incoming Information
nodes.description: Raw text and metadata entering the system for processing.

nodes.label: Capture Phase
nodes.description: Initial ingestion of raw input before any analysis occurs.

nodes.label: Analyze Phase
nodes.description: Diffing against markdown files and Neo4j causal nodes, detecting duplicates and positioning causal chains.

nodes.label: Knowledge Graph & Markdown Corpus
nodes.description: Existing structured knowledge base used as context for evaluating new information.

nodes.label: Orphan Notes & Duplicates
nodes.description: Disconnected or redundant notes that degrade knowledge graph integrity.

nodes.label: Approve Phase
nodes.description: User-facing React UI step where captured and analyzed information is accepted, modified, or rejected.

nodes.label: Contextualized Knowledge Entry
nodes.description: A new note or node properly integrated into the causal graph without broken links or duplication.

nodes.label: Speed vs Depth Tension
nodes.description: The core trade-off between fast capture and thorough analysis that shapes MVP architecture.

nodes.label: Async Batch Processing
nodes.description: MVP architectural choice to capture fast, analyze asynchronously, and approve in batches.

nodes.label: Prompt
nodes.description: Initial input that triggers processing by an LLM

nodes.label: LLM Processing
nodes.description: Edge local or reasoning LLM (e.g., Anthropic) that processes the prompt

nodes.label: Better Utilization
nodes.description: Improved efficiency and usage of resources

nodes.label: Cost
nodes.description: Financial cost associated with LLM usage

nodes.label: Observe
nodes.description: Initial act of gathering raw information from the environment

nodes.label: Knowledge
nodes.description: Accumulated understanding derived from reasoning over summarized content

nodes.label: Evaluate
nodes.description: Assessing observed information for relevance and quality

nodes.label: Predict
nodes.description: Forecasting outcomes based on evaluated information

nodes.label: Decide
nodes.description: Choosing a course of action based on predictions

nodes.label: Act
nodes.description: Executing the decided action in the environment

nodes.label: Feedback
nodes.description: Result of action fed back into the observation loop

nodes.label: Assets
nodes.description: Resources such as skills or money available to an agent

nodes.label: Combined Output
nodes.description: Synergistic result of combining skills and money

nodes.label: Gold
nodes.description: Precious metal serving as a store of value and financial asset

nodes.label: Mining Supply
nodes.description: The quantity of gold produced through mining operations

nodes.label: Portfolio Diversification
nodes.description: Investment strategy of spreading assets to reduce risk, enabled by gold

nodes.label: Central Bank Reserves
nodes.description: Gold holdings maintained by central banks as monetary reserves

nodes.label: Real Interest Rates
nodes.description: Interest rates adjusted for inflation that influence gold's attractiveness

nodes.label: Global Investor Demand
nodes.description: Aggregate demand for gold from institutional and retail investors, driven by risk sentiment, inflation expectations, and currency weakness.

nodes.label: Gold Price Discovery
nodes.description: The market process by which supply, demand, central bank activity, and macro signals are aggregated into a spot price for gold.

nodes.label: Reduced Portfolio Volatility
nodes.description: Lower overall variance in portfolio returns resulting from the low or negative correlation of gold with equities and bonds.

nodes.label: Central Bank Accumulation vs Liquidation
nodes.description: Policy decision by central banks to either increase gold reserves (accumulation) or sell existing holdings (liquidation), each producing materially different effects on gold supply and price.

edges.evidence: Aggregate demand for gold from institutional and retail investors, driven by risk sentiment, inflation expectations, and currency weakness.
The market process by which supply, demand, central bank activity, and macro signals are aggregated into a spot price for gold.
The market process by which supply, demand, central bank activity, and macro signals are aggregated into a spot price for gold.
Lower overall variance in portfolio returns resulting from the low or negative correlation of gold with equities and bonds.
Policy decision by central banks to either increase gold reserves (accumulation) or sell existing holdings (liquidation), each producing materially different effects on gold supply and price.
Accumulation branch: when central banks accumulate gold, reserves increase and upward price pressure is exerted on the gold market.
